cue: Index into the array at two positions.
How many elements does the array hold?
The index can be a variable, not just a literal.
Change a variable in place, then read it back.
Walk i from 0 to a.length and add a[i] to the running total.
note: This loop is just the three atoms stacked: read a[i], run i to a.length, and += into an accumulator.

cue: for...of hands you each value directly, no index; same running total.
syntax: for (const x of a) reads values

cue: Start an accumulator at 0 and add each a[i].
prompt: Implement `sum(a)` returning the sum of the numbers in array `a`. Use a loop; do not use `.reduce`.

cue: Seed an accumulator at 1 and multiply each a[i].
prompt: Implement `product(a)` returning the product of the numbers in array `a`. The product of an empty array is 1.

cue: Combine the elements left to right, carrying a running total from the starting value.
note: reduce is the accumulator loop written as one call: acc starts at the init value, and each step returns the next acc.

cue: Combine the elements with a function, starting from an initial value.
prompt: Implement `sumR(a)` returning the sum of array `a` using `Array.prototype.reduce`.
tests:
if (sumR([1, 2, 3]) !== 6) throw new Error('sumR');
if (sumR([]) !== 0) throw new Error('empty');

cue: Track the index of the best-so-far as you scan.
Hold the best index, update only when a later element beats it.
prompt: Implement `argmax(a)` returning the index of the largest element of `a`; on ties return the first such index. Do not use `Math.max`.

cue: Spread the array into the variadic max.
Bump a counter each time the test passes.
Count elements passing the test with a counter.
prompt: Implement `countEvens(a)` returning how many elements of `a` are even.

cue: Filter to the matches, then read the length.
From index 1, compare each element to its predecessor.
Scan adjacent pairs and bail the moment order breaks.
prompt: Implement `isSorted(a)` returning true if `a` is in non-decreasing order (each element >= the one before).

cue: Compare each element to the one two positions ahead.
Compare each element to the one k positions ahead, watching the bound.
prompt: Implement `countKApartEqual(a, k)` returning the number of indices `i` where `a[i] === a[i + k]`.

cue: Build a fresh array and push each transformed element.
prompt: Implement `squares(a)` returning a new array of each element squared, in order. Use a loop; do not use `.map`.
tests:
if (JSON.stringify(squares([1, 2, 3])) !== JSON.stringify([1, 4, 9])) throw new Error('squares');
if (JSON.stringify(squares([])) !== JSON.stringify([])) throw new Error('empty');

cue: Apply the transform to each element into a new array.
note: This is the push-loop you just wrote: allocate a fresh array, push one transformed element per pass.

cue: Build a fresh array and push only elements passing the test.
prompt: Implement `keepEvens(a)` returning a new array of the even elements of `a`, in order. Use a loop; do not use `.filter`.
tests:
if (JSON.stringify(keepEvens([1, 2, 3, 4])) !== JSON.stringify([2, 4])) throw new Error('evens');
if (JSON.stringify(keepEvens([1, 3])) !== JSON.stringify([])) throw new Error('none');

cue: Keep only elements where the predicate holds.
note: Same push-loop as keepEvens: push only the elements where the predicate holds.

cue: Add to the total only when the guard passes.
Add each element only when it clears the guard.
prompt: Implement `sumPositives(a)` returning the sum of the elements of `a` that are greater than 0.

cue: Outer loop over the sublists, inner loop over their elements.
prompt: Implement `flatten(ll)` returning one array with the elements of each inner array of `ll`, in order (one level deep). Use nested loops; do not use `.flat`.

cue: Collapse the sublists one level deep.
Index both arrays together and accumulate the pairwise products.
Step by index, pushing one from each array per step.
prompt: Implement `interleave(a, b)` returning `[a[0], b[0], a[1], b[1], ...]`. Assume `a` and `b` have equal length.
tests:
if (JSON.stringify(interleave([1, 2], [3, 4])) !== JSON.stringify([1, 3, 2, 4])) throw new Error('interleave');
if (JSON.stringify(interleave([], [])) !== JSON.stringify([])) throw new Error('empty');

cue: Ask whether any element satisfies the test.
Ask whether all elements satisfy the test, minding the empty case.
note: Every element of an empty array passes any test, because there is nothing to fail, so `[].every(...)` is `true`.

cue: Copy first, then reverse the copy; note what happens to the original.
Expand the index/value pairs into an array.
Count the valid start positions for a window of size k.
For each window start, sum the k elements inside it.
prompt: Implement `windowSums(a, k)` returning the sum of each contiguous window of size `k`, left to right. If `k > a.length`, return `[]`.
